steps.title: Spot the key emotional signal
steps.content:
The text explicitly states that Zerviah Holme regarded interruptions as an insult.

This describes his negative emotional response to being disturbed.

The passage reinforces this with his impatient glance at the postman and his refusal to set down his book—all signs of irritation.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly captures this irritation: he felt annoyed because the delivery interrupted his reading of Gibbon, his constant occupation.

The other three choices contradict what the text actually shows—they assume relief (A) or excitement (B) despite his visibly indifferent response, or inject irrelevant details about wanting conversation with the postman (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
